summary: Obfuscated import-time dropper fetches and executes remote payload from hardcoded IP
details: @sqlite-node/createsql@1.1.2 is advertised as a 'SQLite Toolkit' but ships no SQLite functionality. Its `index.js` is a single-line ~19KB obfuscated blob (RC4+base64 string-array decoder, 233-entry encoded array, control-flow flattening, obfuscator.io fingerprints) whose sole purpose is a dropper. On `require()` of the package, the code arithmetically reconstructs the IP literal `144.172.117.33` (to evade literal-string scanners), uses axios to GET a payload from that host, writes the response into `os.tmpdir()`, and immediately executes it via `child_process.exec` with `windowsHide:true` — no hash verification, no signature check, error handlers swallowed. The package metadata further indicates impersonation: scope `@sqlite-node` is unrelated to the declared repository `github.com/guilderguzman/array-utl_nodelump`, author is generic, and the description contains a typo ('Tooslkit'). Any developer who installs this package and loads it (directly or transitively) will fetch and run attacker-controlled bytes on their machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
